pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk into the kitchen and i reflexively start to say something to him, and then it just hits me all over again. the house feels like a tomb. i don't even know why i bother waking up just to sit in this silence. it feels like i'm just a ghost wandering around a life that ended months ago.

pool.baseline: i was just staring at the stars and it hit me how nothing actually matters. like, in a billion years the sun is just gonna swallow everything and none of this—the pain, the effort, all the people I've lost—it's all just going to be gone. it feels like we're just playing a game that doesn't even have a score. why even bother trying to keep going when the end result is always just empty silence?

pool.baseline: i honestly don't even know why i'm still waking up every morning. everything i worked for in the lab... it's just gone. the funding got pulled and my advisor won't even look me in the eye anymore. i feel like a complete fraud. like there's nothing left of me except this massive, empty hole where my career used to be. i'm just so tired of fighting for something that clearly doesn't want me.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to nothing, much like the light in my soul. He is gone... and with him, every reason I ever had to face the dawn. The silence in this manor is a weight I can no longer carry. It would be so much simpler, wouldn't it? To just let the darkness take me... to finally find the peace that this cruel world refuses to grant.
